title: How to Apply for Domicile Certificate Online — State Residency Proof 2026
meta_description: Domicile certificate online 2026 — state-wise application portals, documents, processing time 7-15 days. Required for state government jobs and education.

hero.one_liner: Domicile certificate proves your permanent residence in a state — essential for state government jobs, state university quota, and state-specific schemes

sections.heading: What is Domicile Certificate and When You Need It
content: A domicile certificate is official proof that you are permanently domiciled (resident) in a particular state. It confirms your state of origin or state where you've lived continuously for the required period as per state law.
Unlike an income certificate or caste certificate, domicile is about WHERE you belong, not HOW MUCH you earn or your social category.
Domicile is critical for: (1) State government jobs — most state PSCs, police recruitment, teaching jobs require domicile proof.
(2) State university quota — 85-90% of college seats are reserved for domicile students; without certificate, you cannot access these reserved seats.

(3) State schemes — many schemes are state-exclusive and require domicile (Ladli Behna, Chiranjeevi, Kanya Sumangala, etc.). (4) Agricultural land purchase — some states require domicile for land transactions.
(5) Government contracts/tenders — some state tenders prefer domicile bidders.

sections.caption: Most states now offer online application via e-District portals. Processing time: 7-30 days depending on state and verification backlog.

sections.heading: Domicile vs Residence Certificate — Key Difference

rows.label: Domicile Certificate
rows.value: Proves your PERMANENT home state — where you intend to settle. Valid even if you move to another state temporarily. Permanent/lifetime validity. Used for government jobs, education quota, state schemes.

rows.label: Residence Certificate
rows.value: Proves you CURRENTLY LIVE at a specific address. Valid only for that address. Usually valid for 1-6 months (limited scope). Used for voter registration, local municipal benefits, temporary address proof.

rows.label: When to Use Which
rows.value: Government job/education: Domicile. Local address update: Residence. Scheme eligibility: Check — most state schemes require domicile, some may accept residence. Always verify the specific requirement.

sections.heading: State-Wise Domicile Requirement & Residency Period
rows: Born in UP OR continuous residence 3+ years
Born in Maharashtra OR continuous residence 15+ years
Born in Delhi OR continuous residence 3+ years
Born in Rajasthan OR continuous residence 10+ years (varies by purpose)
Born in MP OR continuous residence 3+ years
Born in Bihar OR continuous residence 5+ years
Born in Karnataka OR continuous residence 10+ years
Native of Tamil Nadu OR continuous residence varies by scheme
Born in Telangana OR residence 7+ years (post 2014 state formation)

steps.title: Visit your state's e-District portal
steps.description: Search '[Your State Name] domicile certificate online' or go to official state website → look for e-District/e-Services section. Common portals: UP (edistrict.up.gov.in), Maharashtra (aaplesarkar.mahaonline.gov.in), MP (mpedistrict.gov.in), Delhi (edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in), Rajasthan (emitra.rajasthan.gov.in).

steps.title: Register and login
steps.description: Create account with email, mobile, password. Some portals accept UMANG app login or DigiLocker login. Once logged in, select 'Domicile Certificate' service under 'Revenue' or 'Administrative' department.

steps.title: Fill application form
steps.description: Form includes: (1) Your full name (as on Aadhaar), (2) Date of birth, (3) Current address in the state, (4) Father's/Guardian's name, (5) Proof of how long you've lived in the state (born OR years of residence), (6) Purpose of domicile certificate (for job/education/scheme). Be clear about your residency claim — 'born in state' is easiest; 'continuous residence for X years' requires documentation.

steps.title: Upload supporting documents
steps.description: Required documents: (1) Aadhaar card (proves identity + current address), (2) Voter ID (proves state registration), (3) Birth certificate (if claiming born in state), (4) Educational certificate (10th/12th marksheet showing school in that state), (5) Electricity/water bill (proves current residence), (6) Ration card (shows address + family), (7) Passport (if applicable), (8) Self-declaration affidavit on ₹10 stamp paper declaring continuous residency in the state, (9) Passport-size photo.

steps.title: Pay application fee
steps.description: Most states charge ₹10-50. Pay online via net banking/UPI/debit card through the portal. Some states waive fees for certain applicants (students, SC/ST). Keep payment receipt.

steps.title: Submit and get reference number
steps.description: Click 'Submit'. You receive a reference/application number (e.g., DC/2026/MH/123456). Save this — use it to track status. You'll also receive SMS/email confirmation.

steps.title: Verification by Patwari/Revenue Officer
steps.description: The SDM office directs Patwari to verify: (1) Is applicant actually born in this state (check birth records)? (2) Has applicant lived continuously in this state for required years? (3) Are Aadhaar/voter ID/school records consistent? (4) Community acceptance in the state. This takes 5-10 days. Patwari may visit your home.

steps.title: SDM approval and certificate issuance
steps.description: After verification, SDM approves. Certificate is: (1) Delivered digitally via DigiLocker (in many states), (2) Downloadable as PDF from portal, (3) Collectible from Tehsil office in printed form. Total processing: 7-15 days.

sections.heading: Proof of Residence — What Counts as Valid Evidence
rows: Proves born in state = permanent domicile
Proves registration in state for 6+ months (voter registration takes time)
Proves studied in state — usually 15+ years before certificate date = long residency
Proves current residence (usually 1-2 month old bills dated to showing continuity)
Proves family residence — but can be transferred, so moderate strength
Proves landowner/resident in state for years (land records are permanent)
Must be supported by other documents. Used with voter ID/Aadhaar for confidence.
Proves employment/study in state since certain date

sections.heading: Special Cases & How to Prove Domicile
content: Case 1: I was born in State A, moved to State B at age 10 and lived there 20 years. Which state's domicile? You can claim domicile of either state.
For State B: you can prove 20 years continuous residence (school, voter ID, Aadhaar, property). For State A: you can claim by birth.
Choose based on where you want government job/education benefits. In practice, most apply in their current state of residence.
Case 2: I was born in State A but have no birth certificate. Can I prove domicile? Yes.

If you have Aadhaar/voter ID from that state, and family members have birth certificates from that state, you can apply. The Patwari will verify from old revenue records that your family is from State A.
Affidavits from elderly family members help.
<strong>Case 3: I moved to a new state 5 years ago. The residency requirement is 10 years.
Can I apply? Not yet. You need 10 years to qualify.

However, check if there's an exception: some states waive residency requirements for people born in that state (even if currently outside), or for children of domicile people. Check state-specific rules.
Case 4: My family owns land in State A but we live in State B. Which state's domicile? Land ownership is secondary.
Your domicile is where you currently reside (State B). If you have continuous residence proof in State B for the required years, apply for State B domicile.
Land ownership in State A doesn't override current residency.

National Portal:
National e-District Portal — Links to all states
DigiLocker:
DigiLocker — Download digital domicile certificates

sections.heading: Domicile vs residence certificate
sections.content: These are different documents. Domicile certificate proves you belong to a state (for quota in admissions/jobs). Residence certificate proves you currently live at an address. Some states issue both as one document, others require separate applications. Check your state's requirement before applying.

sections.heading: Speed up your application
sections.content: Apply online via your state's e-District portal instead of visiting the tehsil office. Online applications get tracked with a reference number, have fixed timelines (usually 15-21 days), and can be downloaded digitally. Offline applications at tehsil offices can take 30-60 days with no tracking.

faqs.question: Is domicile certificate valid for lifetime or does it expire?
faqs.answer: Domicile certificate is valid for lifetime in most states. Once issued, it doesn't expire unless the issuing authority (SDM) explicitly states an expiry date (rare). However, specific institutions (like NIT/IIT during admission) may prefer recently issued certificates (within 1-2 years). Check the specific requirement of the organization/scheme you're applying to.

faqs.question: I was born in UP but migrated to Tamil Nadu 30 years ago. Which state should I apply for domicile?
faqs.answer: Apply for Tamil Nadu since you've lived there 30 years (far exceeds TN's requirement of 7 years for most purposes). You can also apply for UP domicile by birth (optional), but for jobs/education in TN, TN domicile is what you need. Most people apply in their current state of residence.

faqs.question: Can one person have domicile certificates from two states?
faqs.answer: Technically yes, but practically not recommended. If you apply for domicile in State A and later in State B, you have two valid certificates. However, for government jobs/education quota, you can use only one domicile at a time. Check the specific job/scheme — it will ask for domicile of that particular state. Having two creates confusion.

faqs.question: My Aadhaar shows address from 10 years ago but I've lived in current state for 5 years. Will domicile application fail?
faqs.answer: Possibly. The Patwari will question why your Aadhaar shows old address. Update your Aadhaar to current address first (₹50, 10-30 days at UIDAI center or online at aadhar.uidai.gov.in). After updating Aadhaar, apply for domicile. Alternatively, provide other strong evidence (voter ID in new state, current electricity bill, school certificate from current state) along with explanation of address change.

faqs.question: Application under review for 20 days. When will I get certificate?
faqs.answer: Standard processing is 7-15 days. If pending 20 days, check your portal status: (1) If status shows 'Under Verification', wait till day 15-20. (2) If status shows 'Pending Documents', contact Patwari immediately with required docs. (3) If no update for 20+ days, visit the SDM office in person with your reference number and ask for status. Some offices are slow; escalate if needed.

faqs.question: Can I apply for domicile online while currently living abroad?
faqs.answer: It's complicated. Most e-District portals allow online application submission from anywhere, but verification (Patwari visit, community confirmation) may be delayed or refused if you're not present. Some states allow family members to collect on your behalf; others require your presence. Best practice: apply before moving abroad or return to your state for verification, then collect certificate.

faqs.question: Domicile certificate certificate says 'Not Valid for Government Employment'. Why?
faqs.answer: Some states issue limited domicile certificates for specific purposes only (e.g., 'Valid for Educational Quota' but not 'Valid for Government Jobs'). This usually happens when: (1) You requested for a specific purpose, (2) Your residency period is borderline (just meets minimum but not comfortable margin), (3) State's internal restrictions. If you need unrestricted domicile, apply again specifying 'for all purposes' or contact SDM office explaining why you need expanded scope.

faqs.question: Which documents prove domicile fastest — Voter ID or Aadhaar?
faqs.answer: Voter ID is slightly stronger as it's official state-level registration. But Aadhaar is now equally strong and more commonly accepted. Combined (Aadhaar + Voter ID both showing same state address) is the fastest approval. Along with school certificate from that state, approval usually comes in 7-10 days. Without these, processing extends to 15-20 days.

faqs.question: How long is a domicile certificate valid?
faqs.answer: In most states, domicile certificates are valid for a lifetime — they don't expire. However, some states issue them with a validity period (3-5 years) and require renewal. For competitive exam applications, the certificate must be issued before the application deadline. Check your specific state's rules on validity.

faqs.question: Can I get a domicile certificate if I moved to a new state?
faqs.answer: You need to have resided in the state for a minimum continuous period — typically 10-15 years — to get a domicile certificate. The exact duration varies by state. If you moved recently, you'll need domicile from your original state. Some states accept domicile from the state where you passed Class 10 or 12, regardless of current residence.

disclaimer: Domicile certificate requirements, procedures, and validity vary significantly by state. This guide reflects common practices as of March 2026. Always verify specific requirements with your state's revenue department or e-District portal.
